secondary.headline: And he stole the loaf
secondary.description: Written by Fawzi Asaker, editor-in-chief of Al-Alamiya magazine. There was a farmer in my farm who woke up before the rooster crowed, dressed in field clothes, and drank coffee with his virtuous wife. She prepared food for him, carried it in a patch of cloth, and set off for the far field, to spend his day there until sunset, quenching the land’s thirst, trimming the nails of its trees, and sprinkling the sweat of his brow in every direction.

secondary.description: Written by Muhammad Qawwas, “Asas Media” Iran could have played on the pluralism that has been known within the Gulf Cooperation Council since its establishment in 1981. It could have taken advantage of the differences that had recently emerged to fish in turbid waters. She was able to acknowledge the beauty of countries with which she had always had friendly and cooperative relations, to the point that she sometimes received winking exercises.
